questions.question: The unit of energy is measured in ______.
questions.explanation: According to the content, joules are used as a unit of energy.

questions.question: A body at rest has ______ kinetic energy.
questions.explanation: The content states that a body at rest has zero kinetic energy, as there is no motion.

questions.question: The work done by a force on an object can be calculated using the ______.
questions.explanation: According to the content, the work done is given by the product of force and distance.

questions.question: When a force is applied in the opposite direction of an object's motion, the work done is ______.
questions.explanation: The content explains that when a force opposes motion, it does negative work.

questions.question: A kilowatt-hour (kWh) is equal to ______ joules.
questions.explanation: The content provides the conversion factor between kWh and joules.

questions.question: The kinetic energy of an object is directly proportional to its ______.
questions.explanation: According to the content, the kinetic energy of an object depends on the square of its velocity.

questions.question: A car travels ______ kilometers in one hour at a constant speed of 60 km/h.
questions.explanation: The content uses this example to demonstrate the relationship between distance, time, and velocity.

questions.question: When a force is applied parallel to an object's motion, the work done is ______.
questions.explanation: The content explains that when a force helps an object move, it does positive work.

questions.question: The potential energy of an object depends on its ______ and height above the ground.
questions.explanation: According to the content, both mass and height affect the potential energy of an object.

questions.question: A car's kinetic energy is given by ______ × velocity^2.
questions.explanation: The content provides the formula for calculating a car's kinetic energy.

questions.question: When an object is lifted to a greater height, its ______ increases.
questions.explanation: The content explains that potential energy increases with height.

questions.question: A force of 10 N applied over a distance of 5 m does ______ joules of work.
questions.explanation: The content uses this example to demonstrate the calculation of work done.

questions.question: The work-energy theorem states that the net work done on an object is equal to its ______ energy.
questions.explanation: According to the content, the work-energy theorem relates work and change in kinetic energy.

questions.question: A body moving with a velocity of 10 m/s has a kinetic energy of ______ joules.
questions.explanation: The content uses this example to demonstrate the relationship between velocity and kinetic energy.

questions.question: The unit of power is measured in ______ per second.
questions.explanation: According to the content, watts are used as a unit of power.
